Gamers will recognize the value of perfect strafing — moving left and right rapidly and accurately to perform precise gunshots on an opponent. The level of precision that magnetic switches provide far eclipses that of mechanical switches. The Razer Huntsman Mini V3 or the Wooting 60HE are getting a lot of hype for seeming like "cheating" keyboards.

Related
Upgrading these 4 peripherals will make you a better gamer

If you want better gaming performance, your first instinct might be to grab a new graphics card. But don't forget about peripherals.

1 Customize your keyboard without changing switches

Tailor the response when gaming and working

Not everyone cares about being the next esports pro, so let's talk about what magnetic switches have to offer non-gamers. Due to the fundamentally different way magnetic switches trigger a keypress, they "feel" different than mechanical switches. Some people might not like the less tactile response of magnetic switches, while others will praise it for the smoother feedback.

You could fine-tune your switches for rapid response when gaming and increase the actuation distance when working.

Perhaps the biggest benefit of magnetic switches for typing and general use is the level of customization they provide. You can set macros with a single key instead of a key combination. Imagine pressing a key just enough to trigger a regular keypress, and then down all the way to trigger a macro. You can set an additional action for when you release the key, and one more when it passes another adjustable point on the way up.

Imagine fine-tuning your switch settings for a rapid response when gaming, and increasing the actuation distance when working — essentially enjoying multiple keyboard experiences on a single board. Currently, magnetic switch keyboards don't allow the resistance of the switch to be adjusted (since this is dependent on the spring inside it), but this might change pretty soon as manufacturers race to put out the next big advancement in this technology.

Related
Mechanical keyboard modifications: Everything you need to know

Want to take your keyboard experience to the next level? Read on for some simple and not-so-simple mechanical keyboard modifications.

Mechanical vs. magnetic: Is the writing on the wall?

With the obvious advantages of magnetic switches over mechanical ones, is it a foregone conclusion that the battle is over? Some users, especially those who can't go back from the rapid response of magnetic switches, would believe so. On the other hand, people might prefer the distinct feel and sound of mechanical keyboards over these "analog" keyboards. Maybe both of these technologies will co-exist, serving different kinds of users.

Perhaps magnetic switches could even be refined to such an extent that they supersede mechanical switches in every department, sound and feel included. Whatever happens, it sure is an exciting time for keyboard enthusiasts.
item.content:encoded:
Key Takeaways

  • Magnetic switches feature dynamic actuation and reset points; they're faster, smoother, and more durable than mechanical switches.
  • They're unbeatable for competitive gaming, enabling rapid keypresses and unprecedented precision.
  • You could potentially program macros with different actuation points on a single key, and customize the behavior to gaming or typing.

Mechanical keyboards have been responsible for a huge shift in user behavior, owing to the superior typing experience they offer over traditional or membrane keyboards. Mechanical switches are at the core of that experience, offering a satisfying tactile and auditory response to users, which can't be replicated on conventional keyboards.

We are now witnessing what could be another radical shift in switch technology — magnetic switches. To be clear, magnetic switches didn't come up yesterday. The tech has existed since the 60s, and we've seen it in popular keyboards from brands like Wooting since late 2022. However, what's new is the bubbling interest in magnetic switches from consumers and manufacturers, with brands like Wooting and Razer fighting hard to come out on top. Let's see what makes magnetic switches so special.

Related
Akko MOD007B-PC keyboard review: Going on a feature-packed world tour

The Akko MOD007B-PC, with hall effect magnetic switches, a stunning design, and programable RGB lighting, this keyboard is perfect for work and play

3 Magnetic switches are fundamentally different

Mechanical switches could never...

Mechanical keyboards get their name from the type of switch they use. There are a lot of things going on with them, such as the case material, stabilizers, keycaps, foam, and more; but the switches have a greater impact than almost anything else about the keyboard. These switches work by completing an electrical circuit when you press down on a switch. This then triggers the appropriate response which is registered on your computer as you type.

Magnetic switches can have dynamic actuation and reset points, which makes them inherently faster than any mechanical switch.

Magnetic switches (or Hall effect switches) work quite differently — they use a permanent magnet in the switch stem to change the magnetic field when the key is pressed. This change is detected by the PCB and is reflected in the kind of response the system registers. There's no physical contact needed for actuation (i.e. for the system to detect a keypress). This makes magnetic switches more durable than mechanical switches. According to some, they also offer smoother and more consistent keypresses.

However, the most important difference brought about by this magnetic technology is the ability to change the actuation distance on the fly. Unlike mechanical switches that have fixed actuation (keypress detection) and reset points (after which a new keypress can be made), magnetic switches can have dynamic actuation and reset points, which makes them inherently faster than any mechanical switch.

Related
Linear keyboard switches are the best, and I'm tired of pretending they aren't

Clicky and tactile fans, don't get too mad

9

2 Huge advantage for competitive gamers

Enabling rapid keypresses and unprecedented accuracy

So, what do dynamic actuation and reset points have to do with how your keyboard behaves? As it turns out, quite a lot. The biggest "game-changer" from this technology has been shown in competitive gaming, where rapid movements and accuracy are needed to best your opponents. Due to the adjustable behavior of magnetic switches, you're no longer tied to the physical delay that comes with pressing and releasing a mechanical switch.

The level of precision that magnetic switches provide far eclipses that of mechanical switches.

A magnetic switch enables a "rapid trigger" mode which allows a user to reset the switch the moment the key detects an upward movement after a keypress, not requiring the switch to pass a fixed reset point. So, by setting minimal key travel for both the actuation and reset points, you can register far more keypresses in the same amount of time, compared to a mechanical switch.

Besides the reduced physical latency, another benefit of magnetic switches is "rapid strafing." Gamers will recognize the value of perfect strafing — moving left and right rapidly and accurately to perform precise gunshots on an opponent. The level of precision that magnetic switches provide far eclipses that of mechanical switches. The Razer Huntsman Mini V3 or the Wooting 60HE are getting a lot of hype for seeming like "cheating" keyboards.

Related
Upgrading these 4 peripherals will make you a better gamer

If you want better gaming performance, your first instinct might be to grab a new graphics card. But don't forget about peripherals.

1 Customize your keyboard without changing switches

Tailor the response when gaming and working

Not everyone cares about being the next esports pro, so let's talk about what magnetic switches have to offer non-gamers. Due to the fundamentally different way magnetic switches trigger a keypress, they "feel" different than mechanical switches. Some people might not like the less tactile response of magnetic switches, while others will praise it for the smoother feedback.

You could fine-tune your switches for rapid response when gaming and increase the actuation distance when working.

Perhaps the biggest benefit of magnetic switches for typing and general use is the level of customization they provide. You can set macros with a single key instead of a key combination. Imagine pressing a key just enough to trigger a regular keypress, and then down all the way to trigger a macro. You can set an additional action for when you release the key, and one more when it passes another adjustable point on the way up.

Imagine fine-tuning your switch settings for a rapid response when gaming, and increasing the actuation distance when working — essentially enjoying multiple keyboard experiences on a single board. Currently, magnetic switch keyboards don't allow the resistance of the switch to be adjusted (since this is dependent on the spring inside it), but this might change pretty soon as manufacturers race to put out the next big advancement in this technology.

Related
Mechanical keyboard modifications: Everything you need to know

Want to take your keyboard experience to the next level? Read on for some simple and not-so-simple mechanical keyboard modifications.

Mechanical vs. magnetic: Is the writing on the wall?

With the obvious advantages of magnetic switches over mechanical ones, is it a foregone conclusion that the battle is over? Some users, especially those who can't go back from the rapid response of magnetic switches, would believe so. On the other hand, people might prefer the distinct feel and sound of mechanical keyboards over these "analog" keyboards. Maybe both of these technologies will co-exist, serving different kinds of users.

Perhaps magnetic switches could even be refined to such an extent that they supersede mechanical switches in every department, sound and feel included. Whatever happens, it sure is an exciting time for keyboard enthusiasts.

item.title: Ayaneo's new Pocket DMG and Pocket Micro are some of the coolest ways to play retro on the go
item.itunes:title: Ayaneo's new Pocket DMG and Pocket Micro are some of the coolest ways to play retro on the go
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • Ayaneo releases new mobile retro gaming devices, the Pocket DMG and Pocket Micro, with modern upgrades.
  • The Pocket DMG has an OLED screen, a Snapdragon G3x Gen gaming platform, a touchpad, and a 6000mAh battery.
  • Pocket DMG is available on IndieGoGo starting at $339 for early bird models and $419 normally.

Ayaneo has been putting out a lot of good retro-based hardware lately. Don't believe me? Just ask Adam Conway, who gave the Ayaneo Flip DS and Slide a 7.5, and the Retro Mini PC AM02 an 8.5. Now, Ayaneo is ready to release two new products aimed at the mobile retro gamer, the Pocket DMG and the Pocket Micro.

.

Related
Ayaneo Retro Mini PC AM01 review: A mini PC that you can use as a NAS or console

The Ayaneo Retro Mini PC AM01 is a genuinely great mini PC with a lot to love, though there are some weird software kinks at first.

Ayaneo begins taking pledges for the Pocket DMG

Image Credit: Ayaneo

First up, let's crack open the Pocket DMG. The shell is designed to look like a Gameboy Color, except with some nice modern-day upgrades added to it. For starters, it's packing a 3.92'' OLED screen to ensure your retro games look the best they can be. It's powered by the Snapdragon G3x Gen gaming platform and sports a 6000mAh battery to keep you going no matter where you are. It even sports a touchpad on the bottom for finer controls.

If you'd like your own, head over to the IndieGoGo page and make a pledge. If you're quick, you can snap up the early bird models that start at $339. If you missed that, you can still make a normal pledge starting at $419. Finally, once the Pocket DMG goes on sale for retail, the price will begin at $449, so hop on early if you can.

Ayaneo also begins funding for the Pocket Micro

Image Credit: Ayaneo

If the Pocket DMG is a bit too pricey for you, take a look at the Pocket Micro. It's as if a NES controller and a Steam Deck fused into one, with all the charm of the former and the sleek design of the latter. It has a 3.5" 640P IPS display and its tiny size makes it a truly pocket-sized device. If you get in on its IndieGoGo campaign early, you can snap up the early bird price starting at $189. After that, you can make a $199 pledge to get one. Finally, if you wait until retail, prices will start at $219.

If you'd like more information on either device, check out Ayaneo's blog post for more details.

item.title: How to connect a GPU to the PSU
item.itunes:title: How to connect a GPU to the PSU
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
If this is your first time building a PC, and you're wondering how to connect your GPU to the PSU, then you've come to the right place. It may seem daunting to plug your GPU in for the first time, especially if you're new to PC building, but it isn't as complicated as you might think.

However, organizing the rest of your hardware and assembling it in the case is only half the task. The real challenge is to ensure you're getting all the connections correct before you finally get to cable management.

Related
How to make your GPU perform better

Discover key strategies to enhance your GPU's efficiency. Learn about overclocking, cooling solutions, and settings optimization.

Which cables do you need?

The number of cables depends on your GPU's appetite

The first thing you need to know is that a graphics card uses a PCIe cable to draw power from the power supply. A standard PCIe cable has six pins, with a secondary detachable 2-pin connector to form an eight-pin (6+2 pin) connector. Non-modular power supplies will have these cables attached to the PSU, while modular options will allow you to remove them.

I recommend picking up a modular PSU to ensure you don't have to manage any unused cables. Even better, buy shorter cables to reduce the cable slack if you want to take your build to the next level in terms of cable management.

Most modern PSUs on the market these days come bundled with cables that are clearly labeled, so you can't miss the PCIe cable or mistake it for an EPS cable that's used to connect the CPU. Yes, the CPU and GPU use different cables, so keep that in mind.

The pin connectors on the 8-pin EPS12V and the 6+2-pin GPU PCIe cables are shaped differently, so you can't possibly plug the wrong cable even if they're not labeled.

What about the new 12VHPWR connectors?

You'll only find these connectors on Nvidia GPUs for now

The Radeon 7700XT GPU, for example, sports two PCIe connectors, whereas the Gigabyte GeForce RTX 4070 Ti has a 12VHPWR connector. Not all PSUs out there have a 12VHPWR port, though, as only the new ATX 3.0 PSUs include them.

You can, however, use a 12VHPWR adapter to plug the regular 8-pin PCIe cable to draw power for these GPUs, so you don't necessarily have to ditch your old PSU yet. In fact, almost all Nvidia GPUs using the new 12VHPWR connector come with an adapter to make your life easier in any case. If needed though, you can pick up an adapter using the link below.

Cooler Master 12VHPWR adapter cable
$10 at Amazon

How to connect your GPU to your PSU

You'll likely need more than one cable

Now that you've identified the right cable for your graphics card, let's look into how you actually connect everything together.

  1. First, you'll have to locate the PCIe cables on the GPU and the PSU.
    • Each PCIe cable has a 6-pin and a 2-pin connector, meaning it can either be used as a 6-pin cable or combined as an 8-pin cable, depending on the power requirements or the availability of connectors on the GPU.
  2. Plug the required number of PCIe cables into the Power Supply by matching the shapes of the pins. Of course, the PSU should be off, not conducting any live power while connecting components.
  3. Plug the other end of the PCIe cable with the 6+2-pin connector into the GPU by once again matching the shapes.
    • You can combine the connectors by aligning the clips on the connectors, as shown below.
  4. Make sure the connectors are completely inserted. You'll hear a snap once they lock into place. Full contact and latch is important.

Most modern GPUs demand high power to run, so they need more than a single PCIe cable to work. You'll find these connectors on top of the graphics cards, so please ensure you're using the right number of PCIe cables to supply enough power to the GPU. You can also refer to your GPU's specifications to see how much power it needs.

Most modern graphics cards out there will have up to two connectors, meaning you'll need two 8-pin PCIe cables to power them. Nvidia decided to use 12VHPWR connectors on many of its latest GPUs to reduce the number of required connectors, so don't be surprised to see one of those on your GeForce RTX 40-series GPU.

Everything should be connected now!

That's everything you need to know about connecting your graphics card to the power supply. Unless you're using one of the new Nvidia 40-series graphics cards with a 12VHPWR connector, you can simply use the PCIe cables to connect the two components.

Those dealing with a 12VHPWR connector can consider buying an ATX 3.0 PSU with the 12VHPWR connector, or using an adapter (included with the GPU) to power the graphics card. I am leaving links to a couple of my favorite power supply units for gaming on the market right now, so be sure to take a look on your way out.

Cooler Master GX III Gold 750W ATX 3.0
$140 at Amazon

item.title: Here are all the new features added to Copilot for Microsoft 365 in July 2024
item.itunes:title: Here are all the new features added to Copilot for Microsoft 365 in July 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
Key Takeaways

  • IT admins can now enable a dedicated Viva Engage community for early Copilot adopters for support and best practices.
  • Copilot Dashboard no longer requires Viva Insights premium license, just a Copilot for Microsoft 365 membership with requirements regarding seats.
  • End-users can try Copilot in Copilot Lab with Microsoft Graph-powered prompts in Word, PowerPoint, Excel, and use it in Outlook and Forms.

Microsoft has been emphasizing the benefits of Copilot a lot lately, with the AI service being integrated into multiple products including OneNote, Windows 11, OneDrive, and more. Each month, the firm details all the new features it introduced in Copilot for Microsoft 365, and today, it has done the same, covering the month of July 2024.

What's new for IT admins?

As usual, Microsoft's changelog is divided into new capabilities present for IT admins and end-users. We'll start with the former category as well. For starters, IT admins can now enable a dedicated Viva Engage community that's built specifically for early adopters of Copilot. It empowers users to ask questions about the technology, receive support from admins, and get a know-how of the best practices to follow.

Additionally, the Microsoft Copilot Dashboard no longer requires a Viva Insights premium license, you just need to have a Copilot for Microsoft 365 membership with 100 seats or 10 Viva Insights premium licenses assigned. In the same vein, IT admins will be able to utilize SharePoint Advanced Management to manage risk when enabling Copilot to parse their organization's data. Other capabilities that will enhance this data governance infrastructure are the availability of reports for SharePoint sites and site access review.

But what about end-users?

In August, users will be able to give Copilot a whirl in Copilot Lab by using the Try in button, running prompts on Word, PowerPoint, and Excel data, powered by Microsoft Graph. Similarly, Excel customers can leverage Copilot in different ways too, some of which we have discussed in detail here. Outlook users will also be able to draft emails using Copilot and even ask the AI service to better organize their emails.

That's not all, though. Word users can now leverage Copilot to summarize documents with a length of over 80,000 words. Copilot is also deeply integrated into Forms now and can be used to generate quizzes and surveys. Microsoft has emphasized that most of its new capabilities are based heavily on user feedback so customers should continue providing that moving forward too.

item.title: Best cases for Samsung Galaxy S24+ in 2024
item.itunes:title: Best cases for Samsung Galaxy S24+ in 2024
item.itunes:author: XDA[RSS+]
item.itunes:summary:
The Samsung Galaxy S24 series launched in January, and they have since been considered some of the best Android phones on the market. In typical fashion with its flagship series, Samsung unveiled three models: S24, S24+, and S24 Ultra. Of the three phones, the Galaxy S24+ is the perfect option for users who want a bigger display, more RAM, and better storage without going for the Ultra model.

Unlike the S24, which has just 8GB of RAM and up to 256GB of storage, the Galaxy S24+ has 12GB of RAM, up to 512GB of storage, a bigger display, and a larger battery. However, it's still made of the same Gorilla Glass as the other models, meaning you want to attach a case to protect your phone. If you're wondering what cases to get, we've compiled some of the best options on the market listed below.

  • Editor's choice
    Spigen Liquid Air Case for Galaxy S24+
    $17 $30 Save $13

    If you want a slim, basic, and protective case for your new Samsung Galaxy S24+, look no further than this excellent offering from Spigen. It's affordable and barely adds any thickness to your new smartphone. However, it does add a clever triangular grip design to help you hold onto your phone easier. 

    $17 at Amazon
  • Premium pick
    Casetify clear case for Samsung Galaxy S24+

    Show off the beauty of your Galaxy S24+ while keeping it protected with this Casetify clear case. Scratch-resistant and wireless charger-compatible, this case is equipped with UV Defender technology, protecting your device from UV light and staining liquids. It also offers long-lasting clarity, meaning it won't become yellow over time, making it easy to show off your device's cool colors.

    $72 at Amazon $70 at CASETiFY
  • Promoted pick
    SUPCASE Unicorn Beetle Pro case for Samsung Galaxy S24+

    In partnership with Supcase

    The SUPCASE Unicorn Beetle Pro is renowned for being an excellent case series, offering excellent protection and shock absorption. The Galaxy S24+ model carries on the tradition with its built-in screen protector, clicky buttons, raised bezels, texturized edges, and precise cutouts. You also get a kickstand and a free rotating belt clip holster; plus, it has two more colorways you can choose from.

    $27 at Amazon $27 at SUPCASE
  • Best value
    FNTCASE Clear case for Samsung Galaxy S24+
    $9 $10 Save $1

    If you're looking for a solid case at a low price point, the FNTCASE is all that and more. It's a basic clear case with camera protection, raised edges, and a transparent TPU plastic material available for under $10. It's great for people who chose a unique Galaxy S24+ color.

    $9 at Amazon
  • Secure grip
    Poetic Neon case for Samsung Galaxy S24+

    Like many phones with glass backs, the Samsung Galaxy S24+ is susceptible to accidental slips and drops. Adding the Poetic Neon case to your phone reduces the chances of slips as it offers a secure grip, thanks to its anti-slip design. It's also shockproof and scratchproof, and its two-tone color scheme makes for an excellent appeal.

    $17 at Amazon
  • Slim & tough
    Spigen Tough Armor for Samsung Galaxy S24+
    $20 $45 Save $25

    Encasing your Galaxy S24+ in the Spigen Tough Armor offers a better chance of surviving an accidental drop. This case features a dual-layer design with top-notch military-grade protection against bumps, drops, and scratches. It's also functional, supports wireless charging, and features a built-in stand for hands-free viewing.

    $20 at Amazon
  • Impact protection
    Otterbox Commuter Series case for Samsung Galaxy S24+

    The Otterbox Commuter Series case is a slim, tough, and pocket-friendly protector. You get raised edges to keep your camera safe and precise button cutouts that are clicky and responsive. The dual-layer plastic and rubber material helps absorb impact, so your phone has a better chance of surviving a fall.

    $40 at Amazon
  • Official case
    Samsung Official Shield Case for Galaxy S24+

    This official case from Samsung has it all — it'll fit your Galaxy S24 perfectly, it has some style, and it offers protection. You can get it in Deep Violet or Light Gray, and both are excellent colors. If you're looking for a fun way to protect your phone while also making it stand out, look no further.

    $29 at Amazon
  • Deluxe design
    Mous Walnut Protective case for Samsung Galaxy S24+

    Top-tier protection, scratch resistance, and shockproof abilities are what you get from the Mous Walnut Protective case. To make things better, it has a wood accent that makes for a unique and classy look, providing your Galaxy S24+ with that deluxe feel. It has a slim profile and supports MagSafe, so you don't need to take it off when charging wirelessly.

    $70 at Amazon
  • Antimicrobial pick
    Speck Presidio Lux Glitter case for Samsung Galaxy S24+

    The Samsung FlipSuit case is a stylish, innovative case for the Galaxy S24 series. Besides protecting your device from scratches and daily mishaps, this case has a smart card that interacts with your phone to customize your lock screen. You can get more smart cards to match your moods and make your phone even more stylish.

    $45 at Speck
  • Waterproof case
    Spidercase waterproof case for Samsung Galaxy S24+

    Going for a swim and want to keep your Galaxy S24+ protected? Get the Spidercase waterproof case. It's IP68 certified and can keep your phone safe in up to 6.6 feet of water for one hour. This case is also shockproof and scratch-resistant, and the built-in screen protector doesn't stop your fingerprint scanner from working.

    $23 at Amazon
  • Leather case
    Bellroy Leather Case for Galaxy S24+

    To add a premium feel for your new Samsung Galaxy S24+ smartphone, grab the Bellroy Leather Case. It comes in a traditional black or terracotta brown leather material, and simply looks great. On the inside, there's a softer microfiber finish to protect your smartphone. This case doesn't add much in the form of protection, but it sure does look fancy.

    $49 at Amazon $49 at Bellroy

Which case should you choose?

Attaching a solid case to your Galaxy S24+ not only keeps your device safe from scratches, moisture, accidental drops, and bumps but also helps improve your experience, as you won't have to deal with fingerprints, slips, and more. Choosing the ideal case often depends on each person's unique preference, which is why I prefer the Spigen Liquid Air case for Samsung Galaxy S24+ for its lightweight, durable, and protective features.

The Casetify clear case is another option I like due to its unique design, solid construction, and feel. It offers a good grip and supports wireless charging, and its beautiful patterns make for a good conversation starter. For those who want a top-notch case at a low price, the JETech Slim Fit case might seal the deal for you with its solid protection and shock absorption. Ultimately, the cases above are the best picks, whether you want a slim and lightweight case, one that offers top military-grade protection, or a stylish, unique option.

And if you're sitting on the fence, wondering how the Galaxy S24+ compares to its S24 counterparts or other smartphones on the market, our review below will surely be of help.

1:57
Related
Samsung Galaxy S24 Plus review: Get a different phone instead

The Galaxy S24 Plus is not a bad phone at all, it's fine. But it just does not improve enough to justify the $1,000 price.
